This step claims that after applying the push operation and then <br/>alist_of, the resulting pair is (v # fst (alist_of s), snd (alist_of s)). To justify this step, we initially used the lemma alist_of_Abs_bstack_hd_tl to relate the <br/>alist_of and Abs_bstack functions. However, it&prime;s important to note that alist_of_Abs_bstack_hd_tl has an assumption:`assumes &quot;length (fst xs) ≤ snd xs&quot;`<br/>This assumption must be satisfied by the pair `(v # fst (alist_of s), snd (alist_of s))` to apply the lemma correctly. To bridge this gap:<br/>### new lemma3<br/>```isabelle<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>lemma</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> push_preserves_length_constraint: </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>assumes </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">&quot;¬ isfull s&quot;<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>shows </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">&quot;length (fst (v # fst (alist_of s), snd (alist_of s))) ≤ snd (v # fst (alist_of s), snd (alist_of s))&quot;<br/>```<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>[1 more example of request lemma...]</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><br/></font><font New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">bounded_stack_pop_push_inverse:<br/> </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New font.style: font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(237, 237, 237)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;

p.font:
Thought3: Step h4 in the proof: `alist_of (push v s) = (v # fst (alist_of s), snd (alist_of s))`. This step claims that after applying the push operation and then
alist_of, the resulting pair is (v # fst (alist_of s), snd (alist_of s)). To justify this step, we initially used the lemma alist_of_Abs_bstack_hd_tl to relate the
alist_of and Abs_bstack functions. However, it′s important to note that alist_of_Abs_bstack_hd_tl has an assumption:`assumes "length (fst xs) ≤ snd xs"`
This assumption must be satisfied by the pair `(v # fst (alist_of s), snd (alist_of s))` to apply the lemma correctly. To bridge this gap:
### new lemma3
```isabelle

font.style: font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 102, 153), rgb(92, 180, 223)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;
font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(237, 237, 237)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;

font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 153, 102), rgb(37, 168, 125)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;
font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(237, 237, 237)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;

p.font:
"length (fst (v # fst (alist_of s), snd (alist_of s))) ≤ snd (v # fst (alist_of s), snd (alist_of s))"
```

p.font:
To prove the theorem `bounded_stack_pop_push_inverse`, which states that if a stack `s` is not empty and we pop an element from it,..f.
Step 1: Assumptions Given assumptions `a1: \<not> isempty s` and `a2: (v, s0) = pop s`, we know that `s` is not empty and popping from `s` ...
Step 2: Applying `pop` Definition By the definition of `pop`, we have: `pop s = (Some (hd (fst (alist_of s)), Abs_bstack (tl (fst (alist_of s)), snd (alist_of s)))`...
Step 3: Determining `s′` After Pop From the definition of `pop`, we have determined `s′ = Abs_bstack (tl (fst (alist_of s)), snd (alist_of s))`.
Step 4: Applying `push` Definition Now, we need to consider the action of pushing `the v` (the value extracted from `v`) onto `s0`. By the definition of ....
